Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Voter Guide Posts 4–7: rights inventory through 50-year history

Post 4 (Mar 9): Rights inventory mapping ICESCR articles to lived concerns
— job security (Arts 6–7), social security (Art 9), housing (Art 11),
health (Art 12), education (Arts 13–14).

Post 5 (Mar 10): What ratification would actually change — honest limits
(no self-execution, no new spending) vs. what it does create: reporting
obligations, shadow report tools, progressive realization standard,
interpretive authority for domestic courts.

Post 6 (Mar 11): Political map of Senate ratification — SFRC gatekeeping,
the 67-vote threshold, factors predicting treaty support, two-track
advocacy strategy.

Post 7 (Mar 12): The 50-year history — Carter signature 1977, Reagan
reversal, 1992–94 limited window, Obama engagement without ratification,
what the pattern reveals about active political choice.

Add 8 animated themes with CSS animations and particles

New themes with unique visual effects:
- Hacker Terminal: green phosphor CRT, scanlines, blinking cursor
- Schreibmaschine: aged paper, lined cards, ink stamp badges, key-press buttons
- Neon Glow: cyberpunk neon signs, pulsing glow, electric borders
- Pergament: medieval manuscript, ornamental corners, drop caps
- Vaporwave: retro-future grid perspective, gradient text, pink/cyan
- Nordlichter: aurora borealis shimmer, shifting gradient bands
- Unterwasser: floating bubbles, caustic light, gentle card sway
- Kosmos: twinkling star field, nebula glow, deep space
- Feuer: rising ember particles, flickering glow, warm gradients

All animations respect prefers-reduced-motion. Animated themes marked
with star icon in settings. Particle effects (bubbles, stars, embers)
generated via inline JS with CSP nonce.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use correct block tile origin for free plot coordinate conversion

Add BlockXOrigin (-3221) and BlockZOrigin (-221) config entries to
separate the block→world conversion origin from the map display bounds.
Previously used WorldXMin/WorldZMax (display bounds) as the tile grid
origin, causing a systematic offset of ΔX=-341, ΔZ=-861 world units.
Also fix null-safe access to MessageText in ChatHooks.

commit.message: perf: fix LCP - static first hero image outside carousel Root cause: all carousel slides rendered simultaneously in DOM, Lighthouse couldn't identify any single LCP element. Fix: extract first hero image as a static directly in the section, outside the JS carousel — eager + fetchpriority=high, always visible. Slides 2 & 3 stay in carousel div (lazy), animated by JS after 5s. JS rewritten: current=-1 replaced by 3-position rotation [0=LCP img, 1=pancetta, 2=fleur-courgette], dots updated accordingly. feat: rules system — proposal cards, trash zone, support button

Rules panel redesign:
- Decisions renamed to Rules throughout (CSS, JS, HTML, Python)
- Three groups: ACTIVE, DRAFTS, ARCHIVE with drag between them
- Epoch-based remind (replaces flag), honest "Queued — next trigger" feedback
- Soft warning at 7+ active rules, centered helper copy
- Drag-to-delete trash zone in ARCHIVE (reuses Jobs archive-trash-zone)

- Three actions: Activate, Add to drafts, Dismiss (demotes to chat)

- Human-created rules (+ button) go straight to drafts, no card
- Cards use same purple accent as job proposals

Support button:
- Moved from settings to channel bar (far right)
- Pink pill with heart, always visible, not competing with tabs

Fixes:
- Bearer auth for wrapper rule sync
- Browser remind auth header
- Draft → archive drag (deactivate accepts draft status)
- No more "rules: none" injection when no active rules
- Clone bug on draft→trash (make_draft fires edit, not propose)

tilesheet and outputs Rust const string declarations compatible with
sprites.rs. Supports 8x8 and 16x16 source tiles, auto-detects

8x8 to 16x16 automatically. Requires Pillow.

fix: node-level source banding for Color by Source (v1.2.17)

Replace per-link sub-ribbon splitting with node-level source banding.
Each node's [y0,y1] is divided into contiguous per-source bands on both
its left (incoming) and right (outgoing) sides, so like-source ribbons
stack together and flows trace without overlap through all downstream nodes.

commit.message:
feat(fedac): compressor, reverb decay, pink bar, font_1 default, WiFi iface detection, boot skip

Audio: peak compressor (0.7 thresh, 4:1 ratio), reverb damping (0.9997/sample),
period 192 (~1ms), feedback 0.45, buffer clamp. Fixes echo hanging/clipping.

UI: taller pink status bar (14px), "notepat.com" with colored .com suffix,
font_1 (6x10) as default write font, marginless grid buttons for rollover,
full saturation background tint.

WiFi: auto-detect interface name (not hardcoded wlan0), expose iface in JS,
show interface name in WiFi panel for debugging.

Boot: any non-W key skips animation, W-to-install preserved.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Trigger auto-merge workflow for toll freezing fix

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QtQQtMDx3wMHmPXgZCrNEm

* Simplify insurance label to 'Insurance Coverage' in extend modal

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QtQQtMDx3wMHmPXgZCrNEm

* Add weekly/monthly rate buttons to extend rental modal

When a vehicle has pricePerWeek or pricePerMonth configured, the extend
modal now shows quick-select buttons ("1 Week" / "1 Month") that apply
the discounted rate instead of daily pricing. The day counter still
works for custom daily extensions. Savings vs daily rate are shown.

Backend endpoints (extend, create-extension-payment, confirm-extension-payment)
accept a rentalType param to calculate the correct rate.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QtQQtMDx3wMHmPXgZCrNEm

* Fix TollSpot vehicles stuck in pre_registered (Tolls: Pending)

Root cause: The pre-register API returns a success response without a
vehicle ID (the host completes setup on TollSpot's portal). Our code
treated "no ID in response" as incomplete registration, keeping vehicles
stuck as pre_registered forever.

Changes:
- Add extractVehicleId() helper that checks multiple response field names
  (id, vehicle_id, vehicleId, nested under data/vehicle envelopes)
- When TollSpot API returns HTTP 200, mark vehicle as 'registered'
  regardless of whether an ID is present — the API accepted it
- Apply fix to all code paths: vehicle creation, sync, enroll, scheduler
- Add TOLLSPOT_SIGNUP_URL env var support so hosts can be directed to
  complete their TollSpot account setup
- Show contextual "Open TollSpot" link on dashboard when signup URL configured

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QtQQtMDx3wMHmPXgZCrNEm

* Fix toll enrollment button freezing and improve error reporting

- Clear enrollingVehicleId state before showing alert() so the button
  doesn't stay stuck on "Enrolling..." while the alert blocks the UI
- Extract TollSpot error details from multiple response formats (message,
  error, errors, detail, string body) instead of falling back to generic
  axios error message
- Pass TollSpot HTTP status code through to frontend for accurate errors
- Add isTimeout to network error detection in enrollment route

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QtQQtMDx3wMHmPXgZCrNEm

---------

commit.message:
Reclassify BeamtalkInterface and WorkspaceInterface as sealed Object subclass: (BT-1150) (#1200)

## Summary

Reclassifies `BeamtalkInterface` and `WorkspaceInterface` from `Actor
subclass:` to `sealed Object subclass:` (value types). These classes
have no backing gen_server, no mutable state, and no OTP process — the
Actor declaration was incorrect.

Linear issue: https://linear.app/beamtalk/issue/BT-1150

## Key Changes

- **BeamtalkInterface.bt / WorkspaceInterface.bt**: Changed `Actor
subclass:` → `sealed Object subclass:`, replaced `@primitive` methods
with Erlang FFI (`(Erlang M) fn: arg`)
- **beamtalk_primitive.erl**: Added `BeamtalkInterface` and
`WorkspaceInterface` to `module_for_value/1` for tagged-map dispatch
routing
- **beamtalk_interface_primitives.erl**: Added direct FFI exports
(`findClass/1`, `allClasses/0`, `globals/0`, `help/1`, `help/2`,
`version/0`) for ErlangModule proxy calls
- **beamtalk_workspace_interface_primitives.erl**: Rewrote as value-type
dispatch with direct FFI exports; fixed ETS table ownership bug (table
now owned by long-lived `beamtalk_workspace_bootstrap` process, not
short-lived eval workers)
- **beamtalk_workspace_config.erl**: Split `singletons/0` into actor
singletons and `value_singletons/0`; removed both classes from
supervisor child specs
- **beamtalk_workspace_bootstrap.erl**: Create ETS table and
`code:ensure_loaded(beamtalk_interface_primitives)` at init so atom
table is populated before first dispatch
- **beamtalk_workspace_sup.erl**: Removed actor child specs for the two
reclassified classes
- **generated_builtins.rs**: Marked `BeamtalkInterface` and
`WorkspaceInterface` as sealed value types in the semantic analyzer

## Test Plan

- [x] `just test` — all 856 BUnit tests + 2938 Erlang runtime unit tests
pass
- [x] `just test-e2e` — all e2e integration tests pass (incl.
`tcp_bind_as_updates_bindings_test`,
`tcp_bind_as_available_in_next_eval_test`)
- [x] `just ci` — full CI green

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* New public runtime APIs: list/find classes, view globals, get version,
list/inspect actors, load code, bind/unbind values, and create/init
bindings table.
* WorkspaceInterface adds a class-level current accessor to get/set the
active workspace instance.

* **Changes**
* BeamtalkInterface and WorkspaceInterface converted to sealed
value-style objects (no longer actor processes); bootstrapped and stored
differently.
* Workspace bindings moved to a centralized, long-lived bindings table
and new readiness semantics.

* **Tests**
* Tests updated to reflect value-singleton behavior, new instantiation
patterns, and revised workspace sentinel/ETS handling.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files, enabling CodSpeed CI integration for deterministic performance
regression tracking.

- CMake: dual-path dep — upstream Google Benchmark locally, CodSpeed
  v2.1.0 when CODSPEED_MODE is set (instrument-hooks requires Clang)
- All 7 bench files: nanobench API → RegisterBenchmark + DoNotOptimize,
  section/name hierarchy via "/" separators, preambles to stderr
- Scripts: bench_all.sh uses --benchmark_out JSON directly, removed
  parse_bench.py dependency; analyze_bench.py and generate_charts.py
  updated for Google Benchmark JSON schema
- New .github/workflows/codspeed.yml for CodSpeed CI (OIDC auth)
- Updated benchmarks.yml to capture JSON output natively
